circular_title: CBDT Circular: TDS Rate Revision - FY 2026-27
circular_summary: TDS rates revised for Section 194C and 194J effective April 2026.
actions: Update ERP or payroll-accounting systems to apply the revised TDS rates from April 2026 for contractual payments under Section 194C and professional or technical fee payments under Section 194J

Verify PAN availability, threshold limits, and the nature of payment before deciding the applicable section and rate for contractual payments under Section 194C and professional or technical fee payments under Section 194J
Identify any short deduction arising from legacy rate tables through vendor ledger review and prepare correction statements where return filings need amendment
Maintain section-wise documentation for contractor payments, consultancy fees, and technical service invoices to support classification under Section 194C or Section 194J
email_subject: Compliance Advisory: CBDT Circular on TDS Rate Revision - FY 2026-27
email_body:
Amit Verma, Galaxy Securities Pvt. Ltd.,

This advisory is specifically applicable to your company as a stock broker, as you are required to deduct TDS on contractual payments under Section 194C and professional or technical fee payments under Section 194J.

As per the CBDT Circular: TDS Rate Revision - FY 2026-27, we recommend the following actions to ensure compliance:

1. Update your ERP or payroll-accounting systems to apply the revised TDS rates from April 2026 for contractual payments under Section 194C and professional or technical fee payments under Section 194J.
2. Verify PAN availability, threshold limits, and the nature of payment before deciding the applicable section and rate for contractual payments under Section 194C and professional or technical fee payments under Section 194J.
3. Identify any short deduction arising from legacy rate tables through vendor ledger review and prepare correction statements where return filings need amendment.
4. Maintain section-wise documentation for contractor payments, consultancy fees, and technical service invoices to support classification under Section 194C or Section 194J.

The deadline for completing these actions is April 26, 2026. Please note that non-compliance may attract penalties, although the circular does not specify the exact amount.

If you require any assistance or clarification on these actions, please do not hesitate to contact us. We are here to support you in maintaining compliance with tax regulations.

Best regards,
Compliance Advisory Team
internal_notes: CA notes — deadline was estimated, cross-checks needed for client-specific compliance profile
